sourceMovie.title: Heatwave
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Heatwave for the same reason it broke through in 2022: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ernie Barbarash keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heatwave clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heatwave clicked for you, Unfaithful (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Heatwave?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Unfaithful, Disclosure—each shares DNA with Heatwave while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Heatwave?
faq.answer: Heatwave (2022) is best described as Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Heatwave worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
